feedback.text: Dear Madam, Sir, I think this initiative is very promising, and I am hoping it provides us with a better experience online. I would like to suggest adjacent areas to the ones proposed here, where the commission could also protect the consumer as well as improve the market. My first suggestion relates to video games, I would like to see the commission requiring that video games that will be sold on the European market are developed with an end of life plan in mind. So that a consumer can still play the games they have bought after a company deemed it not profitable anymore to maintain it, and decides to stop all support and associated cost. I would love for this to apply to any game where a transaction has occurred, be that a single purchase transaction, a subscription or a micro transaction. My other suggestion would be to try and level the playing field for streaming services. A lot of the video streaming services are creating small monopolies by relying on exclusive access to specific intellectual properties. As a result the costs for the consumer is increased because they must register and pay fees for several products providing the same service. Which is, as I understand it, the opposite of what is expected from a competitive market. Note that this doesn't seem to be as bad for music streaming services, maybe because they tend not to be in the business of producing their own music. They maybe a good case study for legislation. These micro monopolies also remove any incentive for the companies to provide or improve the user's streaming experience past a minimal "good enough" point, since they compete on their catalog rather than on the design of the platform. I found myself having paid for such a service (and denied a refund) and being unable to watch any media because it does not appear to work on any of my computers. Finally and related to this, it is becoming very difficult to buy and own these sort of digital media, and I would love to see the streaming platforms be required to provide a "buy and download" option next to their streaming product so that the consumer has a choice in how to spend their money. Your faithfully, Quentin Lambert
